titleStmt.title: Saudi Arabia 2022 – Speaker 10 – Marriage (Part 1)
WIBARAB Corpus
titleStmt.funder: Funded by the European Research Council under the Grant Agreement 101020127

publicationStmt.distributor: Austrian Center for Digital Humanities and Cultural Heritage

recording.p: Recorded during Saudi Arabia Fieldwork Campaign Gunda Kinzl 2022

prefixDef.p: Private URIs using the teiCorpusHeader prefix are pointers to any element in the WIBARAB teiCorpus document.
Private URIs using the share prefix are pointers to audio file residing on the WIBARAB network share.
Private URIs using the geo prefix are pointers to the xml:id attribute on a place element in VICAV Geodata list.
Private URIs using the dmp prefix are pointers to the xml:id attribute on an element in the WIBARAB DMP document..
Private URIs using the zotid prefix are pointers to the xml:id attribute on a biblStruct element in the TEI export of the VICAV Zotero Group library.
Private URIs using the sources prefix are pointers to the xml:id attribute on an element in the WIBARAB list of sources.

spanGrp.span: for example, nowadays when one of my daughters gets married, or one of sisters or one of my relatives, we would tell her a thousand congratulations and may God aid you and give you luck
and we would advise her according to what the Prophet had said, may the blessings of Allah be upon him
you would be like a mother to him and he would be like a slave to you ((accusative in umman and ʕabdan))
may the blessings of Allah be upon the prophet Muhammed, and from everyone money and from him money and from you (as a woman) children, God willing

For example nowadays if one of daughters got married I give her advice((mafʕul muṭlaq)), How she should be like a mother you should be like a mother, (how to be) sympathetic towards him and kind, a friend to him, how to take care of him like how his mother would take care of him, and he would be like a slave to you
meaning he will act with you like he would obey his mother, for example, he would become like a slave to you, do you understand?

yes, and from him money is expected, (he is expected to provide the money), that he pays the obligations of marriage and the expenses and from her children (are expected), from her children, to provide sons and daughters, do you understand?
and this is all according to the current waves, as Muslims not as Rashayda members in particular
